immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped body-temperature measurement event representing event-level ICU body temperature rather than a baseline, fever flag, summary, or stay-level aggregate. Exact-key raw temperature-site context may be retained when available, but the variable still means the temperature event itself.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_temp must preserve this event-level body-temperature observation meaning.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range for the governed MIMIC build, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: A later review may lock a body-temperature semantic anchor after the cross-database event boundary is frozen more formally.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_temp from event-level body temperature to fever-status classification
changing std_temp from event-level body temperature to first-day or admission baseline temperature
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same event-level body-temperature meaning
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing fever flags or derived thermal classifications as std_temp

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_temp MVP execution should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_temp outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
